en: Does your company have vessels under US Coast Guard regulations?
Does your company operate cargo vessels of 500 gross tons or greater?
International Maritime Organization International Safety Management Code
Does your company maintain ISM Code (International Management Code for the Safe Operation of Ships and for Pollution Prevention) certification?
Does your company have written Health, Safety, and Environmental (HSE) policies and procedures for marine vessels?
Are your (HSE) policies and procedures for marine vessels located both at your shoreside headquarters and aboard each vessel?
Does your HSE program include written procedures for the safe operation of vessels?
Does your HSE program include written procedures for pollution prevention?
Does your HSE program include written procedures for reporting shipboard incidents and non-conformance to shoreside management?
Explain your method for ensuring that the objectives of your HSE policies are achieved.
Explain how your company verifies that your HSE policies are implemented at all levels of the company, both shoreside and aboard all vessels.
How does your company ensure that safety and environmental protection responsibilities are communicated to and understood by vessel personnel and shoreside personnel?
Does your company give your Vessel Master(s) primary responsibility and authority for ensuring safety aboard their vessels?
If you answered yes to the previous question, is this communicated in writing?
How does your company monitor your operations to ensure that HSE policies and procedures are being followed aboard your vessels?
Explain your method for ensuring that vessel personnel are medically fit for their work activities.
Explain your method for ensuring that vessel personnel are properly trained and licensed for their work activities.
How does your company ensure that new personnel or personnel receiving new vessel assignments are given proper familiarization with the vessel and their shipboard duties?
If you answered the previous question, is this familiarization process documented?
Do all vessels have detailed written procedures that address safety and environmental protection for key shipboard operations?
If you answered yes to the previous question, do these procedures clearly identify and assign the personnel who are qualified to perform specific functions?
What procedures are in place for identifying and responding to shipboard emergencies (i.e., fire, man overboard, abandon ship)?
How often do your vessels conduct fire drills?
How often do your vessels conduct man overboard drills?
How often do your vessels conduct abandon ship drills?
Does your company have a program in place to ensure that your shoreside headquarters can respond to a vessel emergency at any time?

Does your company have a program in place to ensure that non-conformances, accidents, and hazardous situations (near misses, spills, etc.) occurring during maritime operations are reported to shoreside management, investigated, and responded to with appropriate corrective action?
Does your company have a program in place to ensure that each vessel's equipment and machinery are properly maintained in accordance with manufacturer specifications and applicable regulations?
Does your preventative maintenance program identify and give special consideration to certain critical equipment to avoid hazardous situations if it should fail?
If you answered yes to the previous question, how does your company ensure that all vessels maintain the necessary documentation onboard?
Does your company conduct internal audits onboard vessels and at shoreside headquarters?
If you answered yes to the previous questions, explain your company's method for ensuring company-wide implementation of the corrective actions necessary to address audit findings.
Has your company undergone a shoreside ISM Code Audit?
If yes, when was your last shoreside ISM Code compliance audit conducted?
If no, what is your target date to resolve them?
Has your company undergone a vessel ISM Code Audit?
If yes, when was your last vessel ISM Code Audit conducted?
If no, what is your target date to resolve them?
Does your company own or operate any marine vessels?
Does your company ensure that vessel personnel are properly trained and licensed?
Does your company ensure that vessel personnel are medically fit to perform their work activities?
